meta.name: Business Licenses & Regulations
meta.department: Finance / departments (general business) + Department of Liquor Control (liquor)
meta.description: govOS free public service: a plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 5 (Business Licenses & Regulations) — how to get a business license, liquor licensing, short-term-rental cross-references, and who pays. Sourced to Municode + County of Maui; hands off to MAPPS / Liquor Control. First version, expanding.
meta.pageTitle: Title 5 — Business Licenses & Regulations · govOS · Kilo Aupuni
meta.eyebrow: 12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 5 (Business Licenses & Regulations): how to get a business license, how liquor licensing works (a separate Department of Liquor Control track), the short-term-rental cross-references you can't skip, and who pays. General business licenses go through MAPPS; liquor through Liquor Control — this service explains the law and hands you off to the official systems. Sections marked expanding are being filled in from the ingestion corpus; nothing here is legal advice.

sections.title: ★ Parcel lookup
sections.note: Running a business from a property? Your zoning district decides whether the use is even allowed there before any license matters — a home business (Title 19, ch. 19.67) and a short-term rental (ch. 19.65) each have zoning rules on top of the Title 5 license. Enter a Maui County TMK to see the parcel's zoning and designations, live from the Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS.

source.html: Live designations: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. Business licenses via Maui County MAPPS. No parcel data is stored or fabricated — fetched live.
source.expanding: use-allowed overlay for home business / STR

sections.title: 1 · Get a business license
sections.note: General County business licenses go through MAPPS (which replaced the legacy KivaNet system).

steps.name: Confirm the use is allowed
steps.what: Check your zoning first (Title 19) — a license doesn't override zoning. Home businesses (ch. 19.67) and short-term rentals (ch. 19.65) have separate zoning requirements.
steps.pay: Expense: free to check · paid by: you (time)

steps.name: Create a MAPPS account & apply
steps.what: Register for MAPPS Customer Self-Service, open the Business Licenses module, complete the steps and upload the required documents.
steps.pay: Expense: license fee · paid by: applicant

steps.name: Review & issuance
steps.what: The County reviews the application against Title 5 requirements and issues the license; some regulated activities route to additional review.
steps.pay: Expense: County review · paid by: County (+ applicant fee)

steps.name: Renew & comply
steps.what: Keep the license current and comply with the conditions for your activity (and any state license, e.g. GET, separately).

position.label: ⚖️ Position — James Langford (analysis, not law; a stance beside the rule)
position.text: A business license should be a low-friction registration that lets a resident earn a living, not a revenue barrier. Where the County requires a license, the process should be fast, online, and cheap — the burden of compliance shouldn't fall hardest on the smallest operator.

source.html: County of Maui For Business + MAPPS — Business Licenses (replaced KivaNet). MCC Title 5.

sections.title: 2 · Which license or regulation?
sections.note: Title 5 covers general business licensing and several regulated activities; liquor is a separate department.

rows.v: Liquor license — Dept. of Liquor Control

source.html: MCC Title 5 (§§5.06–5.26) + County Liquor Control.
source.expanding: full chapter list (peddlers, specific regulated trades)

sections.title: 3 · Liquor license classes
sections.note: Liquor is regulated by the Department of Liquor Control (its own rules, MC-08), not the general business-license track. Classes include:

source.html: County of Maui Department of Liquor Control + Liquor Control Commission; rules Title MC-08. Apply via the DLC (separate from MAPPS).

sections.title: 4 · Short-term rentals — the cross-reference you can't skip
sections.note: An STR is the classic trap: a business license alone is not enough.
sections.html: Operating a short-term / vacation rental on Maui touches three titles at once: Title 19 zoning decides whether it's even allowed and on what permit (short-term rental homes, ch. 19.65; B&Bs ch. 19.64), Title 3 places it in the TVR-STRH tax class (a higher rate), and Title 5 business licensing applies to the operation. Clear all three before advertising — see the Title 19 and Title 3 services.

source.html: MCC Title 5 (STR/TVR licensing cross-refs, per the MCC inventory) + Title 19 ch. 19.65 + Title 3 classification (TVR-STRH).

sections.title: 5 · Enforcement
sections.note: How an unlicensed or non-compliant business is addressed. Not legal advice.
sections.html: Title 5 carries its own licensing requirements and remedies, backed by the County's general penalty provisions (Title 1) for code violations; liquor violations are enforced separately by the Department of Liquor Control (license suspension/revocation + fines under its rules). Note: the Title 19 enforcement chapter (§19.530.030) reaches MCC titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 — it does not list Title 5, so business-license enforcement runs on Title 5 / Title 1 / Liquor-Control tracks, not the zoning-enforcement engine.

source.html: MCC Title 5 + Title 1 (general penalty) + Liquor Control. Specific Title 5 penalty sections expanding pending verbatim ingest.

sections.title: 6 · Who pays — the charter lens
sections.note: Labeled analysis — licensing read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, the County licenses and regulates businesses through Finance/departments, with the Department of Liquor Control a dedicated regulator; license fees are set by ordinance / rule. The Charter frames licensing as a regulatory and revenue function delegated to the administration.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, the right to earn an honest living is foundational; a license is the County's permission to exercise a pre-existing right. The lens reads licensing as something that should be minimal, fast, and cheap for residents — with regulation focused where there is real public risk (alcohol, STR housing impact), not used as a general toll on small enterprise.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Regulate where there's genuine public risk — alcohol, short-term-rental pressure on housing — and make everything else a two-minute online registration. The fee should recover processing cost, not gate the resident out of their own livelihood. Same license, read two ways.

sections.title: 7 · Rules → Code: candidates for codification
sections.intro: Provisions that, in our assessment, should be codified / streamlined in Title 5 so the binding standard and cost are readable from the Code. Citations are verbatim-checked; the recommendation is a stance beside the rule.
items: Single published fee schedule. License fees are spread across rules and practice. → Codify a one-page Title 5 fee schedule so any operator can read their cost up front. expanding

STR cross-title coordination. An STR clears Title 19 + Title 3 + Title 5 separately, with no single front door. → Codify a unified STR registration that confirms zoning, tax class, and license together.
Small-operator on-ramp. The smallest businesses bear the same process as the largest. → Codify a simplified track (low-risk activities) so licensing doesn't deter resident enterprise.

source.html: Analysis grounded in MCC Title 5 + the County business & liquor licensing framework; contents expanding pending verbatim ingest.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this page, the live parcel lookup, the licensing navigator, and the license/liquor-class tables are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a license-readiness checklist, an STR cross-title checker, and an AI licensing assistant will be verification-gated (identity-verified). Identity is verified through the payment processor — only verified-status is stored here, never the raw ID. No live billing is enabled (Stripe keys are environment references only); the actual license application happens on MAPPS or with Liquor Control.
